description: Per sample processing for 2 audio channels

properties.note: UI-only: controls whether the container is displayed vertically or horizontally in the scriptnode graph. No effect on audio processing.

gaps.question: Is frame2_block identical to frame1_block in processing logic except for fixing channel count to 2 instead of 1? Does the child's processFrame() receive a span<float, 2> with both channels interleaved?
gaps.context: If identical except for channel count, the doc can reference frame1_block and note the stereo difference. This is the most common frame container since most networks are stereo.

gaps.question: With frame<2, T>, each call to processFrame() receives span<float, 2> containing [left, right]. Is the interleaving done by FrameConverters::forwardToFrameStereo() or by the generic toFrameData() path? Are there any SIMD optimisations for the stereo case?
gaps.context: Stereo frame processing is the most common use case. Understanding the exact mechanism helps explain performance characteristics.

notes: Variant of frameN_block group with N=2 (stereo). Uses wrap::frame<2, T> = wrap::fix<2, wrap::frame_x<T>>. This is likely the most commonly used frame container since most scriptnode networks are stereo. The existing phase3 doc is a stub redirecting to frame1_block. References frame1_block exploration for shared logic (CPU overhead, bypass, per-sample concept).
